expectations.text: Report contains PMID 30910320
expectations.evidence: Present throughout

expectations.text: Report includes at least one author name from the paper
expectations.evidence: Full author list with affiliations

expectations.text: Report describes the study design
expectations.evidence: Investigator-initiated, randomised, double-blind, placebo-controlled, multicentre trial

expectations.text: Report includes sample size (n)
expectations.evidence: 631 screened, 201 randomised

expectations.text: Report is concise, does NOT contain multi-leg comparative table
expectations.evidence: 147 lines — nearly 3x longer than with_skill (51 lines). Includes NNT analysis, subgroups, AIM commentary

expectations.text: Report includes primary endpoint result with effect size

notes: More thorough but less concise
Includes subgroup analysis not requested
with_skill achieves 100% pass rate across ALL 3 evals (20/20 assertions) vs 61.1% without (12/20)
Biggest delta: eval-2-slide-id (100% vs 42.9%) — multi-leg pipeline adds most value for slide evaluation with depth rubric, GRADE, cross-ref, and synthesis
Smallest delta: eval-3-fast-path (100% vs 83.3%) — fast-path routing is simpler, baseline also performs well
Discriminating assertions (never pass without skill): comparative-table, convergence-divergence, depth-rubric, grade-assessment, multi-leg-findings, comparative-synthesis

Non-discriminating assertions (pass both): verified-pmids, pmid-present, author-present, study-design, sample-size, effect-size, slide-read, mcp-query-results — both configs handle basic data retrieval well
with_skill costs +33% time (+64.7s) and +24% tokens (+14283) — significant but justified by +39pp pass rate gain
fast-path routing works correctly: with_skill produces 51 lines vs 147 without — skill constrains scope appropriately
PubMed MCP instability affected eval-1 without_skill (5 failed attempts), but eval-1 with_skill recovered via Leg 5
